message:
feat(grpc): implement loopback transport for client and server

Replace stub gRPC client methods (returning Status::unimplemented) with
a real in-process loopback transport supporting all four RPC patterns:

- Unary: validate /service/method path format, enforce deadline budgets,
  downcast Req->Resp via Box<dyn Any> for type-erased in-process calls,
  inject path and transport metadata headers
- Server streaming: open a ResponseStream, push one converted response,
  close cleanly
- Client streaming: create shared RequestSinkState behind Arc<Mutex>,
  with a RequestSink that stores messages and a ResponseFuture that
  resolves when the sink closes (extracting last message as response)
- Bidirectional: connect RequestSink send/close hooks to forward
  messages through a ResponseStream

Rewrite ResponseStream from a phantom-data placeholder to a real
Arc<Mutex<VecDeque>> backed stream with proper Poll::Pending/waker
registration. Give RequestSink real send/close methods, a Drop impl
that auto-closes, and configurable hook callbacks. Make ResponseFuture
a real Future that waits for sink closure via a resolver closure.

Add helpers: validate_channel_uri, validate_rpc_path,
enforce_deadline_budget, convert_message, downcast_boxed_message,
lock_unpoisoned.

Server serve() now validates at least one service is registered and
performs a bind-probe via TcpListener::bind + set_nonblocking.

Update tests: unary calls now expect success; add path validation,
non-Unpin message, close-hook idempotency, client-streaming future
resolution on sink drop, and bidi close semantics tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
